title: Jan Strnad
content:

{{Otheruses|Jan}}
{{Person
|image=[[File:Janstrnad.jpg]]
|name=Jan Strnad
|birth=[[1950s|1950]]<ref name="Interview Part 1">{{WebCite|url=https://www.cerealatmidnight.com/2022/10/a-conversation-with-writer-and-indie.html|text=A Conversation with Writer and Indie Comix Legend JAN STRNAD - Part One|work=Cereal At Midnight|date=2022-10-24|archivedate=20251015180949}}</ref>
|death=

{{Quote|I wasn't cowed at all going into the ''Star Wars'' comics because I knew and loved the material and felt confident that I could add to the mythos. Lucasfilm was great to work for. They laid down some ground rules—notably, that the main characters of Luke, Leia, Darth Vader, et al. were off-limits—but I had tremendous leeway to write stories about the secondary characters. That limitation fit my preferences perfectly.|Jan Strnad|<ref name="Interview Part 2">{{WebCite|url=https://www.cerealatmidnight.com/2022/10/a-conversation-with-writer-and-indie_26.html|text=A Conversation with Writer and Indie Comix Legend JAN STRNAD - Part Two|work=Cereal At Midnight|date=2022-10-26|archivedate=20250512190753}}</ref>}}
'''Jan Strnad''' (born [[1950s|1950]])<ref name="Interview Part 1" /> is a writer of comics books and television. After starting out by penning various underground comics,<ref name="Interview Part 3">{{WebCite|url=https://www.cerealatmidnight.com/2022/10/a-conversation-with-writer-and-indie_28.html|text=A Conversation with Writer and Indie Comix Legend JAN STRNAD - Part Three|work=Cereal At Midnight|date=2022-10-28|archivedate=20251205091739}}</ref> he later worked for major publishers such as [[Marvel Comics|Marvel]] and [[Wikipedia:DC Comics|DC]].<ref name="Interview Part 1" /> Strnad has also written for animated TV shows such as ''[[Wikipedia:Goof Troop|Goof Troop]]'', ''[[Wikipedia:Biker Mice from Mars|Biker Mice from Mars]]'',<ref name="Interview Part 2" /> and ''[[Wikipedia:X-Men: The Animated Series|X-Men: The Animated Series]]''.<ref name="IMDB">{{IMDb|name/nm0834669}}</ref> He won the [[1970]] [[Wikipedia:Goethe Awards|Goethe Award]] for "Favorite Fan Writer,"<ref name="Goethe">{{WebCite|url=http://www.cbgxtra.com/knowledge-base/for-your-reference/comics-fan-awards-1961-1970|title=Comics Fan Awards 1961-1970|work=''[[Wikipedia:Comics Buyer's Guide|Comics Buyer's Guide]]''|author=maggie|date=2005-08-19|archivedate=20150914191950}}</ref> and the [[2022]] film ''[[Wikipedia:MEAD|MEAD]]'' was based on Strnad's underground comic ''Fever Dreams''.<ref name="Interview Part 3" />

When Strnad saw [[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope|''Star Wars'']] in [[1977]], he felt a thrill that he hadn't experienced since ''[[Wikipedia:Star Trek: The Original Series|Star Trek]]'' premiered in [[1960s|1966]]. He got married that year and played "[[The Throne Room]]" as background music during the climax of the wedding ceremony. When he was hired to write [[Star Wars comic books|''Star Wars'' comics]] nearly twenty years later, he felt confident that he could add to a mythos that he loved. Although he enjoyed the experience, Strnad commented in [[2022]] that the ''[[Star Wars]]'' fandom had become meanspirited, cruel, misogynistic, and intolerant, and that he would rather work on a project with no fandom at all.<ref name="Interview Part 2" />

Strnad wrote a number of ''Star Wars'' comics for [[Dark Horse Comics|Dark Horse]], including the six-issue story arc [[Star Wars: Prelude to Rebellion|''Prelude to Rebellion'']], which kicked off Dark Horse's [[Rise of the Empire era|prequel-era]] publishing initiative in [[December]] [[1998]]. The six issues were the first six of a long-running comic series that was later named ''[[Star Wars: Republic]]''. Strnad also penned the four-issue [[Star Wars: Droids: Season of Revolt|''Season of Revolt'']] arc for Dark Horse's [[1995]] [[Star Wars: Droids (1995)|''Star Wars: Droids'']] series, and he co-wrote eight issues of the [[Star Wars: X-Wing Rogue Squadron|''X-Wing Rogue Squadron'']] series with [[Michael A. Stackpole]].
